prerequisites: A Stripe account with access to the Developers Dashboard (dashboard.stripe.com) so you can view and create API keys.

A Stripe secret API key (prefix sk_live_ or sk_test_) or a restricted API key (prefix rk_live_) created from the API keys tab in the Dashboard. The connector reads this value into the required client_secret field.
The Stripe account identifier (prefix acct_) for the account you want to read data from, supplied as the required account_id config field.
Decide whether to read live data or sandbox/test data: live-mode keys (sk_live_ / rk_live_) return real account data, while test-mode keys (sk_test_) return simulated objects only.

authMethods.name: Secret or restricted API key (client_secret)
authMethods.summary: Stripe authenticates requests using HTTP Basic Auth, with the API key provided as the basic-auth username and no password. Put your secret key (sk_live_ / sk_test_) or restricted key (rk_live_) into the connector's client_secret secret field. Stripe recommends restricted API keys for production because they grant only a configurable subset of permissions, limiting damage if the key is exposed. Secret keys are shown only once at creation, so copy and store the value immediately.

setupSteps.title: Open the API keys tab in the Stripe Dashboard
setupSteps.body: Sign in to the Stripe Dashboard and go to the Developers > API keys tab (dashboard.stripe.com/apikeys). This is where you reveal existing keys and create new ones. Choose test mode or live mode depending on whether you want simulated or real account data; the toggle changes the key prefix (sk_test_ vs sk_live_).

setupSteps.title: Create the API key
setupSteps.body: Stripe recommends a restricted API key (prefix rk_live_): follow the Restricted API keys flow to assign only the permissions your read needs. To create a full secret key instead, click Create secret key, complete the email or text verification, name the key, then click Create. The secret value is displayed only once, so copy it right away and store it securely (it cannot be retrieved later).

setupSteps.title: Provide the key as client_secret
setupSteps.body: Paste the copied API key into the connector's required client_secret field. The connector sends it to Stripe as the HTTP Basic Auth username on every request, exactly as Stripe's authentication guide describes.

setupSteps.title: Set the account_id
setupSteps.body: Enter your Stripe account identifier (prefix acct_) in the required account_id field so the connector targets the correct account.

setupSteps.title: Tune optional sync settings
setupSteps.body: Adjust the optional config fields as needed: start_date sets the earliest data to read; lookback_window_days re-reads recent records to capture late updates; slice_range controls the time window per request; num_workers and call_rate_limit govern concurrency and request pacing; api_retention_streams selects streams subject to Stripe's data-retention limits. Leave these unset to use defaults.

sources.title: Stripe Docs - Making API calls for connected accounts (acct_ ID)
